target.name: Agility Robotics
target.founder: Jonathan Hurst, Damion Shelton, Mikhail Jones

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $385M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $2.0B - $2.3B.

private.name: Divergent
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series E), post-money $2.3B

data.narrative:
Agility Robotics, a Series E robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $400M+, is valued here against a comparable universe of 9 private and 1 public peer company. The three most relevant private comparables—Divergent (Series E, $2.3B), Exotec (Series D, $2.0B), and Locus Robotics (Series F, $2.0B)—span the same sector and adjacent financing stages.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis uses $385M in total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.4x step-up multiplier typical for Series E advancement, yielding $539M. Peer-median analysis across the three same-sector comparables produces a $2.0B implied value, with a stated range of $2.0B to $2.3B. These two methods diverge substantially, reflecting differences in how stage maturity and peer benchmarking weight the valuation.

Triangulating both methods yields a range of $458M (low) to $2.3B (high), with a median estimate of $1.3B. The wide spread reflects meaningful uncertainty: stage-progression anchors to disclosed capital raised but may underweight peer growth trajectories, while peer-median relies on a limited three-company sample in a rapidly evolving sector. Key sensitivities include revenue traction relative to peers and nearness to profitability or Series F funding. The median estimate of $1.3B serves as a reasonable central point pending deeper revenue and unit economics diligence.
